content: DECISION (issue #6, fine-tuning verbs): unsloth + its torch stack is a BASE runtime dependency in pyproject [project].dependencies, NOT an optional extra and NOT a subprocess. Product-owner (Ori) ruling: uv is the default installer, 'uv tool install unsloth-cli' must bring the full tuning stack. This REVERSES the previously load-bearing 'dependencies = []' / zero-runtime-deps rule that CLAUDE.md documents in multiple places (Zero runtime dependencies, Architecture, forward-work) — those sections must be rewritten when the verbs land. Mitigations kept: handlers LAZY-import torch/unsloth inside the function body (never module top-level) so introspection verbs (whoami/learn/explain/doctor/overview/cli) stay fast and the afi rubric gate (teken cli doctor --strict) stays green (the gate checks the CLI contract, not dependency count). Known traded-away property + risk: 'uv tool install unsloth-cli' now FAILS entirely on any arch where torch/unsloth wheels don't resolve (verify Spark ARM/Blackwell wheels). Other confirmed decisions: FLAT verbs (sloth train/eval/export, not a 'tune' noun group); run-config = TOML via stdlib tomllib; controlling skill = /finetune (validate -> train -> eval -> export). Two dataset schemas validated before GPU: chat {messages:[{role,content}]} and task {task,input,expected_output}. Spec: docs/specs/2026-06-26-unsloth-cli-ships-spark-friendly-lora-qlora-adapte.md

VERIFIED on hardware (2026-06-26): the base-dependency decision for unsloth-cli's fine-tuning verbs (see unsloth-cli-finetune-base-dep-reversal) HOLDS on the DGX Spark (ARM/Blackwell). `uv lock` + `uv sync` resolve and install unsloth 2026.6.9 + torch 2.10.0 cleanly (138 packages, incl. xformers/triton/bitsandbytes and the MLX stack); `uv run sloth whoami` runs. So `uv tool install unsloth-cli` bringing the full tuning stack is viable on Spark. Shipped in PR #? (branch spec/finetune-lora-issue-6) bumping to v0.4.0: flat sloth train/eval/export verbs + /finetune skill + dependency-free sloth/tune/ core (datasets/config/metadata/scope) + lazy _trainer.py. 170 tests, 92.67% coverage, afi rubric gate green.

GOTCHA (PR #8, SonarCloud quality gate): the gate failed on new_security_hotspots_reviewed=100%, blocked by a python:S5852 ReDoS SECURITY HOTSPOT on a regex in sloth/tune/scope.py. Lesson: S5852 is a *hotspot raised for review*, not a code issue — it fires regardless of regex form. Possessive quantifiers (\d++, the documented Sonar remediation) did NOT clear it and cost a full CI cycle. Clearing a hotspot requires marking it reviewed in SonarCloud, which needs a project token CI does not expose to agents. RELIABLE fix: ELIMINATE the regex so the rule cannot fire — replaced re.compile(...).findall with a hand-rolled scan (_scan_number_span + _is_param_token), verified behaviour-identical on the model-name corpus. Repo rule: to pass the Sonar gate on a security hotspot via code alone, remove the flagged construct entirely rather than making it provably-safe-but-still-flagged. Also: the gate-failing CI job is named `test` (it runs sonar-scanner); the SonarCloud check status only appears once that job finishes.
